model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Louisville holds a decisive advantage in both offensive and defensive efficiency metrics, ranking 18th in KenPom and 16th in Torvik compared to South Florida's 50th in both, with a significantly stronger adjusted offensive efficiency (124.1 vs. 117.7) that should exploit the Bulls' average defense. While South Florida has a solid 24-8 record and a credible path through disciplined play and rebounding, Louisville's superior talent, experience in the ACC, and proven ability to execute in high-pressure situations make them the more reliable pick. The underdog's path would require a perfect shooting night and Louisville having an off game, which is unlikely given the metrics and recent form.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Team Quality and Matchup Specifics
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated Louisville and South Florida's advanced metrics, recent form, and potential injury concerns to determine the most likely winner.
reasoningStep.evidence: Louisville ranks 18th in KenPom and 16th in Torvik with elite offensive efficiency (adj_o 124.1) and solid defense (adj_d 98.7), while South Florida ranks 50th in both with average efficiency (adj_o 117.7, adj_d 101.9). No significant injury news was found for either team via web searches, suggesting both are at full strength. Louisville's ACC schedule and superior metrics provide a clear edge over South Florida's American Athletic Conference competition.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
